x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Instruction Validation Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rules that determine whether a booking instruction is valid before processing.

Includes:
Verification of account numbers and transaction types
Authorization checks (signatures, approvals)
Compliance with product-specific rules

Comment:
Example:
A loan repayment instruction is only processed if the loan account is active and authorized.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Transaction Posting Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Guidelines on how transactions are mapped to ledger accounts and posted.

Includes:
Debit and credit account mapping
Fee allocation
Interest accrual posting
Handling of reversals

Comment:
Example:
Processing a term deposit interest accrual: debit interest expense, credit customer account.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Cut-off and Timing Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies when transactions can be booked, including daily or periodic cut-offs.

Comment:
Examples:
End-of-day processing at 5:00 PM

Transactions after cut-off posted next business day

Batch processing schedules

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Fee and Charge Application Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rules that determine what fees or charges apply to bookings and how they are calculated.

Includes:
Transaction fees
Service charges
Late payment penalties

Comment:
Example:
A €2 fee applies for each manual transfer booking.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Correction Policy Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rules that govern how errors are corrected or reversed after booking.

Includes:
Authorization required for corrections
Time limits for reversals
Audit logging of changes

Comment
Example:
Duplicate transaction can be reversed within 24 hours with supervisor approval.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Exception Handling Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how transactions that cannot be booked normally are handled.

Includes:
Suspicious transaction flagging
Transactions with insufficient funds
Error logging and manual review

Comment:
Example:
A payment exceeding the account balance is flagged for manual processing.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Regulatory Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Ensures that booking practices comply with legal and regulatory requirements.

Comment:
Examples:
Anti-money laundering (AML) checks

Central bank reporting requirements

Statutory ledger and accounting standards

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Notification Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specifies when and how customers or stakeholders are notified about booked transactions.

Includes:
Confirmation messages
Alerts for failed transactions
Periodic account statements

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Settlement Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rules for finalizing and settling transactions between accounts or external parties.

Includes:
Interbank settlements
Clearing house instructions
FX transaction settlement

x-enumeration-literals.name: Booking Authorization Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rules that define who can approve or authorize bookings, especially for high-value or exceptional transactions.

Comment:
Examples:
Manager approval for transactions above €50,000

Dual authorization for manual bookings

Online authentication for electronic bookings
